Decision: train a 1-epoch full fine-tune with the same validated setup and submit it to map the early-training target curve around the current best 2-epoch checkpoint.

trajectory.description: Observation: early full fine-tuning peaks around the 2-epoch checkpoint (0.1835) and longer/shorter full fine-tuning underperforms, suggesting CLIP features are useful but updating the backbone quickly overfits source Sketch. Analysis: preserving the pretrained CLIP representation while training only the classifier head may reduce target-domain drift; because the head is random, it can be trained for several epochs cheaply with a larger learning rate. Decision: run a frozen-backbone Sketch-only classifier-head experiment for 8 epochs and submit it to test whether less backbone adaptation improves Quickdraw accuracy.

trajectory.description: Observation: freezing the CLIP backbone with the default full-finetune learning rate (3e-5) failed badly (0.0192 Quickdraw, low source val), meaning the random classifier head barely learned. Analysis: this does not invalidate frozen CLIP features; the LR was inappropriate for training a new linear head. A frozen-head-only CLIP linear probe should use a much larger LR, and if it learns a clean Sketch classifier while preserving pretrained CLIP features it may generalize better than full fine-tuning. Decision: rerun frozen-backbone Sketch-only training with LR=1e-3 for 12 epochs and submit evaluation.

trajectory.description: Observation: best remains the 2-epoch full CLIP fine-tune on Sketch (0.1835); longer full fine-tuning overfits and frozen linear probing fails badly, so preserving CLIP while doing limited adaptation is key. Analysis: the current augmentation includes color jitter and horizontal flips, which may be suboptimal for Quickdraw’s black-on-white line drawings; a more target-like augmentation should force grayscale/high-contrast line-style inputs while still allowing small geometric variation. Decision: add a transform mode focused on Quickdraw-style sketch preprocessing/augmentation, train a short 2-epoch CLIP full fine-tune on Sketch with the previously successful LR, and submit evaluation to test whether target-style augmentation improves over the baseline.

Analysis: adjacent checkpoint averaging is beneficial; a more diverse checkpoint trained with Quickdraw-style augmentation scored lower alone (0.1759) but may add complementary decision boundaries. Decision: create a 3-model ensemble manifest using baseline 2e, 3e, and quickdraw-augmentation 2e checkpoints with the validated TTA path, then submit evaluation.

trajectory.command:
sed -n '1,260p' train.py && printf '
--- model.py ---
' && sed -n '1,220p' model.py && printf '
--- manifests ---
' && for f in checkpoints/*.json; do echo $f; cat $f; done && printf '
--- checkpoints ---
' && ls -lh checkpoints
